identity.name: SMB/RPC Lateral Sweep — Internal Host Discovery (T1046 / T1018 / T1021.002)
identity.summary: A single internal host rapidly opens TCP connections to SMB/RPC ports (445/139/135) across many internal hosts in a short window — the classic pre-lateral-movement reconnaissance sweep. The CortexSim smb_rpc_sweep EAL plugin reproduces this across the ITDR lab subnet. East-west NGFW traffic logs feed XSIAM Network Security Analytics, which detects the high destination-host fan-out from one sour…
identity.description: Before lateral movement, operators (and worms/ransomware affiliates) sweep the internal network to find reachable SMB/RPC services — TCP/445 (SMB), TCP/139 (NetBIOS session) and TCP/135 (RPC endpoint mapper). The signature on the wire is a single source contacting an unusually large number of distinct internal destinations on those ports within a short window. The detection surface for Cortex is east-west network telemetry: NGFW (or internal sensor) traffic logs record src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port and app per session, and XSIAM Network Security Analytics models the destination-host fan-out per source against the admin/scanner baseline. This card detects the sweep from panw_ngfw_traffic_raw / network_story using count_distinct(dst_ip) per src_ip on SMB/RPC ports, plus an SMB-app behavioural BIOC for traffic outside the admin baseline. A correlation rule fuses the fan-out and the SMB-anomaly signals, and an analytics-plane stitch ties the sweeping source IP back to its workstation/endpoint context in XSIAM so the SOC sees one lateral-recon incident instead of hundreds of connection logs.

execution.framework_reference_id: T1046 / T1018 — CortexSim smb_rpc_sweep EAL plugin (1 src → many internal dst on 445/139/135)

prerequisites.description: Lab environment matching scenario SIM-NDR-004 requirements (east-west NGFW/internal sensor logging the ITDR lab subnet to XSIAM).
NGFW/internal sensor forwards east-west traffic logs into XSIAM (panw_ngfw_traffic_raw / network_story carries src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port).

payload.code:
# step-1: Dry-run: verify CIDR coverage and authorisation
cat > /tmp/smb-sweep-dry.yml <<'YML'
campaign_id: CMP-NDR-004
name: "NDR validation — SMB sweep dry"
authorized_by: "domain-consultant@paloaltonetworks.com"
simulation_authorized: true
target_allowlist:
  - 10.50.10.0/24
dry_run: true
tags: [ndr, lateral, eal]
steps:
  - step_id: step-01
    plugin: smb_rpc_sweep
    params:
      target_cidr: "10.50.10.0/24"
      ports: [445, 139, 135]
      connect_timeout: 1.0
      inter_host_delay: 0.05
      max_hosts: 64
YML
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/smb-sweep-dry.yml
# step-2: Live SMB/RPC sweep against ITDR lab subnet
cat > /tmp/smb-sweep-live.yml <<'YML'
campaign_id: CMP-NDR-004
name: "NDR validation — SMB sweep live"
authorized_by: "domain-consultant@paloaltonetworks.com"
simulation_authorized: true
target_allowlist:
  - 10.50.10.0/24
dry_run: false
tags: [ndr, lateral, eal]
steps:
  - step_id: step-01
    plugin: smb_rpc_sweep
    params:
      target_cidr: "10.50.10.0/28"
      ports: [445, 139, 135]
      connect_timeout: 1.0
      inter_host_delay: 0.1
      max_hosts: 16
YML
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/smb-sweep-live.yml --live

expected_artifacts.description: One source IP connecting to many distinct internal destinations on TCP/445 in a short window.
SMB/RPC connection attempts on ports 445, 139 and 135 from a single source.
SMB application traffic from a non-admin source outside the normal admin baseline.

biocs.name: NGFW EAL — anomalous SMB traffic outside admin baseline
biocs.description: NGFW EAL — anomalous SMB traffic outside admin baseline
biocs.logic:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter dst_port in (445, 139, 135) or app in ("smb", "ms-ds-smb", "msrpc", "netbios-ss")
| filter src_ip != null
| comp count_distinct(dst_ip) as dst_host_count, count() as session_count by src_ip, dst_port
| filter dst_host_count >= 8
| fields src_ip, dst_port, dst_host_count, session_count

xql_queries.name: Dry-run report shows planned host count and ports
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_port in (445, 139, 135)
| filter dst_ip in ("10.50.10.0/24")
| comp count_distinct(dst_ip) as planned_hosts by src_ip, dst_port
| fields src_ip, dst_port, planned_hosts

xql_queries.name: NGFW EAL — host sweeping pattern (1 src to many dst on TCP/445)
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_port = 445
| filter dst_ip in ("10.50.10.0/24")
| comp count_distinct(dst_ip) as swept_hosts, count() as sessions by src_ip, dst_port
| filter swept_hosts >= 8
| fields src_ip, dst_port, swept_hosts, sessions

xql_queries.name: XSIAM correlation — sweep src host stitched with workstation context
xql_queries.query:
preset = network_story
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_port in (445, 139, 135)
| filter dst_ip in ("10.50.10.0/24")
| join type=inner (xdr_data
    | filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK
    | fields agent_hostname, src_ip as ep_src_ip) as ep ep.ep_src_ip = src_ip
| comp count_distinct(dst_ip) as swept_hosts by src_ip, ep.agent_hostname
| filter swept_hosts >= 8
| fields src_ip, ep.agent_hostname, swept_hosts

correlation_rules.name: SMB RPC Lateral Sweep — Host Fan-Out From One Source
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(NGFW EAL — anomalous SMB traffic outside admin baseline) OR XQL(NGFW EAL — host sweeping pattern (1 src to many dst on TCP/445)) OR XQL(XSIAM correlation — sweep src host stitched with workstation context)

analytics_modules: Cortex XSIAM Network Security Analytics — Internal Network Scan / Host Sweep
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — Lateral Movement reconnaissance over SMB/RPC

products.evidence_query: dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw | filter dst_port in (445,139,135) | comp count_distinct(dst_ip) by src_ip
preset = network_story | filter dst_port = 445 | comp count_distinct(dst_ip) as swept by src_ip | filter swept >= 8
Playbook: isolate the sweeping host, quarantine its AD account, enumerate reached hosts, open lateral-movement DFIR ticket.

use_cases.name: Lateral Reconnaissance Detection via NGFW EAL — SMB/RPC Sweep
use_cases.description: Validate that east-west NGFW traffic logs + XSIAM Network Security Analytics detect a single source sweeping SMB/RPC ports across many internal hosts.

test_cases.objective: Host sweep fan-out on TCP/445 is detected from east-west telemetry.
preconditions: East-west NGFW/internal sensor logging the ITDR lab subnet to XSIAM.
steps: Run SIM-NDR-004 step-02 (live sweep of 10.50.10.0/28 on 445/139/135).
Inspect Network Security Analytics / network_story for the source IP.
success_criteria: XQL `NGFW EAL — host sweeping pattern (1 src to many dst on TCP/445)` returns >= 1 row with swept_hosts >= 8.
BIOC `NGFW EAL — anomalous SMB traffic outside admin baseline` matches the source.

test_cases.objective: Sweep is stitched to the originating workstation and raised as one incident.
success_criteria: XQL `XSIAM correlation — sweep src host stitched with workstation context` returns >= 1 row.
Correlation `CR-NDR-0004` raises a single lateral-recon incident for the source.

preventive_controls: Segment internal networks and apply default-deny east-west policy so SMB/RPC is only permitted from sanctioned management hosts.
Disable SMBv1 and restrict TCP/445/139/135 reachability between workstations.
Require host-based firewall rules denying inbound SMB except from approved admin jump hosts.
detection_engineering: Baseline per-source destination-host cardinality on SMB/RPC ports; alert on fan-out above the admin baseline.
Distinguish sanctioned scanners/admin hosts from anomalous sources via an allowlist.
Stitch the sweeping source IP to its endpoint/workstation context and correlate into one lateral-recon incident.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK T1046 — Network Service Discovery
